oldest_week.activity_summary: Major repository restructuring completed with backend directory moved to asmcomp, plus runtime issues addressed
oldest_week.activity:
- **Repository restructuring** completed by moving entire `backend/` directory structure to `asmcomp/` location, affecting hundreds of files including compiler internals, architecture-specific code, and debugging infrastructure
- **Runtime compatibility** addressed with [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) reporting OCAMLRUNPARAM=v=4096 flag overloading issue where custom timestamp behavior conflicts with upstream OCaml 5.1 changes ([#3158](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3158))
- **Flambda2 development** progressed with [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) closing continuation lifting issue after implementation completion ([#2413](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/2413))
- **Compiler optimizations** delivered through multiple PRs including string scope refactoring ([#3117](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3117)), directory flag refactoring ([#3120](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3120)), and debuginfo comparison improvements ([#3119](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3119))
- **Type system enhancements** completed with layouts documentation for contravariance ([#3144](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3144)) and unboxed vector types mode crossing fixes ([#3138](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3138))
- **Performance improvements** achieved through array reference kind refactoring ([#3152](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3152)) and block header field reordering reversion ([#3145](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3145))
oldest_week.trends_summary: Focus on compiler infrastructure reorganization and runtime system improvements
oldest_week.emerging_trends:
- **Compiler architecture consolidation** with major directory restructuring moving backend functionality into asmcomp for better organization
- **Runtime system refinement** addressing compatibility issues between custom extensions and upstream OCaml changes
- **Flambda2 optimization maturity** with continuation lifting and other advanced compiler optimizations reaching completion
- **Type system evolution** continuing work on layouts, modes, and unboxed types for performance improvements
oldest_week.contributors_summary: Two contributors active with issue reporting and closure
oldest_week.contributors:
- [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) reported runtime parameter compatibility issue with OCAMLRUNPARAM=v=4096 flag behavior conflicting with upstream OCaml 5.1 changes ([#3158](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3158))
- [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) closed the continuation lifting issue after successful implementation in Flambda2 optimization pipeline ([#2413](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/2413))

oldest_week.brief_summary: Enhanced locate command with context overrides and merged major OCaml 5.2.0minus-1 changes plus tooling improvements
oldest_week.new_features_summary: New `-context` flag for locate command enables precise namespace searching and automated magic number update script
oldest_week.new_features:
- Enhanced **locate command** with new `-context` flag allowing explicit namespace specification via values like `expr`, `module_path`, `type`, `constructor`, `label`, etc., overriding context inference ([#91](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/91))
- New **magic numbers update script** (`update-magic-numbers.sh`) for automated OCaml version compatibility maintenance, automatically syncing magic numbers from upstream OCaml flambda configuration ([#108](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/108))
- Fixed **EXCLUDE_QUERY_DIR directive** handling for cmt/cms files, ensuring proper directory exclusion behavior during indexing ([#104](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/104))
- Improved **ghost location handling** in occurrences query by switching from ghost location filtering to `Location.none` filtering, better supporting let punning and PPX-generated code ([#110](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/110))
oldest_week.activity_summary: Multiple infrastructure improvements including OCaml 5.2.0minus-1 merge and cleanup of unused magic numbers

- **Locate command enhancement** completed by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) adding flexible context override capability for precise namespace searches, enabling users to specify exact identifier search contexts rather than relying on position inference ([#91](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/91))
- **OCaml 5.2.0minus-1 integration** delivered through comprehensive upstream merge by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923), bringing latest flambda backend changes while temporarily deferring parameterized module support ([#106](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/106))
- **Development tooling improved** with new automated magic numbers update script by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923), streamlining OCaml version compatibility maintenance with validation and error checking ([#108](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/108))
- **Code cleanup completed** by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) removing unused magic numbers from configuration, reducing maintenance overhead and potential confusion ([#107](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/107))
- **Bug fix implemented** by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) for EXCLUDE_QUERY_DIR directive handling in cmt/cms files, with intention to upstream the fix to main OCaml merlin ([#104](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/104))
- **Occurrences query improvement** merged by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) switching from ghost location to `Location.none` filtering, coordinated with related [ocaml-flambda/flambda-backend#3137](https://github.com/ocaml-flambda/flambda-backend/issues/3137) changes ([#110](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/110))

latest_week.brief_summary: liburing updated to 2.14, new zero-copy and io_uring ops exposed, GC registration bug fixed.
latest_week.new_features_summary: New io_uring ops (send_zc, sendmsg_zc, recv_zc, epoll_wait, pipe, etc.) and Setup_flags (hybrid_iopoll, cqe_mixed, sqe_mixed) added.
latest_week.new_features:
- Fixed **GC registration bug** in `ocaml_uring_setup` where the `polling_timeout` (`int option`) parameter was not registered with the GC via `CAMLparam`, which could cause memory corruption when the GC moves values; reported by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) ([#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/135)) and fixed by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136))
- Added **`send_zc` and `sendmsg_zc` zero-copy send operations** (`IORING_OP_SEND_ZC`, `IORING_OP_SENDMSG_ZC`) to the capability discovery system in `discover.ml` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139))
- Added **seven further new io_uring operations** to `discover.ml` as part of the liburing 2.14 update: `IORING_OP_RECV_ZC`, `IORING_OP_EPOLL_WAIT`, `IORING_OP_READV_FIXED`, `IORING_OP_WRITEV_FIXED`, `IORING_OP_PIPE`, `IORING_OP_NOP128`, and `IORING_OP_URING_CMD128` by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- Added **four new `Setup_flags`** to the OCaml API in `uring.mli`: `no_sqarray` (fail on older indirect submission systems), `hybrid_iopoll` (delayed I/O completion polling to reduce CPU usage), `cqe_mixed` (mixed 16/32-bit responses to save memory), and `sqe_mixed` (mixed 64/128-bit requests to save memory), with documentation pointing to `io_uring_setup_flags(7)` ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- Updated **vendored liburing to version 2.14** (from the previous version), incorporating 305 changed files with new operations, examples, tests, and man pages for features like `io_uring_resize_rings`, `io_uring_register_region`, zero-copy receive (`zcrx`), and `io_uring_submit_and_wait_min_timeout` by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- Updated **README.md documentation** with instructions for updating liburing to new versions, covering the `git subtree pull` workflow and required edits to `dune` and `discover.ml` ([#137](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/137))
latest_week.activity_summary: All four PRs opened this week were merged: GC bug fix, build docs, liburing 2.14 update, and new zero-copy ops.
latest_week.activity:
- **GC safety fix** for `ocaml_uring_setup` landed same-day after [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) reported that the `polling_timeout` parameter was missing from `CAMLparam` registration, merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136), closes [#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/135))
- **Build process documentation and simplification** merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), with a note that the subtree scheme was previously broken and a follow-up update was already planned ([#137](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/137))
- **Vendored liburing updated to 2.14** in a large squashed subtree merge, followed immediately by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) adding new operation and flags enums to the OCaml wrappers ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- **Missing `send_zc` and `sendmsg_zc` ops added** to `discover.ml` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), which were absent from the capability discovery list despite being present in liburing ([#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139))
latest_week.discussions_summary: Vesa Karvonen identified and reported the GC registration bug, which was promptly acknowledged and fixed.
latest_week.notable_discussions: - [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) filed [#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/135) identifying that `ocaml_uring_setup` used `CAMLparam1(entries)` but the `polling_timeout` argument (an `int option`) was not registered, making it unsafe under GC pressure; [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) responded with a fix PR within the hour and the issue was closed the same day ([#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136))
latest_week.trends_summary: Rapid tracking of upstream liburing releases with same-week OCaml API surface updates for newly exposed operations.
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- The **liburing 2.14 update** followed by immediate additions to `discover.ml` and `uring.mli` demonstrates a pattern of keeping the OCaml bindings closely aligned with upstream liburing releases, exposing new kernel capabilities quickly ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138), [#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139))
- **Zero-copy networking operations** (`send_zc`, `sendmsg_zc`, `recv_zc`) are being actively surfaced in the OCaml API, reflecting growing interest in high-performance I/O paths ([#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139), [#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
latest_week.contributors_summary: Thomas Leonard drove the liburing 2.14 update and GC fix; Anil Madhavapeddy added missing zero-copy ops; Vesa Karvonen reported the GC bug.
latest_week.contributors:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened and merged three PRs: the `ocaml_uring_setup` GC registration fix ([#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136)), build process documentation improvements ([#137](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/137)), and the liburing 2.14 vendor update with accompanying OCaml API additions ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened and merged a PR adding the missing `send_zc` and `sendmsg_zc` operation entries to the capability discovery system ([#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139))

latest_week.brief_summary: Added missing_ok option to Eio.Path.unlink; draft PR opened for io_uring performance flags.
latest_week.new_features_summary: Eio.Path.unlink gains optional ~missing_ok parameter to silently ignore missing files.
latest_week.new_features: - **`Eio.Path.unlink`** gains an optional `~missing_ok` parameter: when set to `true`, the function silently does nothing if the target path doesn't exist rather than raising an `Fs.Not_found` IO error ([#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833), resolves [#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828)). This mirrors the existing `~missing_ok` behaviour in `rmtree` and avoids the boilerplate of catching `Not_found` at call sites.
latest_week.activity_summary: Draft PR for io_uring performance flags opened; incremental directory reading PR received design feedback.
latest_week.activity:
- **io_uring performance flags** draft PR opened by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#840](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/840)), proposing `IORING_SETUP_DEFER_TASKRUN` and `IORING_SETUP_SINGLE_ISSUER` flags for `eio_linux` which showed a large speed improvement on HTTP benchmarks; currently blocked by the benchmark CI machine running Linux 5.15 which is too old to support these flags.
- **Incremental directory reading** draft PR ([#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)) by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) received design input from [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), with the discussion favouring a non-recursive `walk` API that hands off descending logic to the caller, enabling future parallel or sequential traversal primitives to be built on top.
latest_week.discussions_summary: Design discussion on incremental directory walk API: non-recursive approach preferred to keep primitives composable.
latest_week.notable_discussions: - **Walk API design** for [#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821): [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) discussed whether `Eio.Path.walk` should recurse automatically or yield a single directory's entries and leave recursion to the user. The agreed direction is the non-recursive form, so a separate parallel or sequential recursion helper can be layered on top without baking the choice into the primitive.
latest_week.trends_summary: Incremental improvements to the filesystem API continue, with both ergonomics (missing_ok) and performance (io_uring flags) in focus.
latest_week.emerging_trends:
- The **filesystem API** is receiving incremental ergonomic improvements, with `missing_ok` on `unlink` ([#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833)) following the same pattern already present in `rmtree`, suggesting a broader push toward consistent optional-error semantics across path operations.
- **io_uring backend performance** is an active concern, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) exploring newer kernel flags ([#840](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/840)) and [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) working on **incremental directory reading** ([#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)) that already shows 10-13x speedups on Linux.
latest_week.contributors_summary: Volker Diels-Grabsch contributed the merged feature; Thomas Leonard reviewed, merged, and opened a new draft; Patrick Ferris and Anil Madhavapeddy advanced the walk API design.
latest_week.contributors:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) authored and submitted PR [#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833) adding `~missing_ok` to `Eio.Path.unlink`, originally proposed in issue [#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828).
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) reviewed and merged [#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833), closed issue [#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828), and opened the draft PR [#840](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/840) exploring io_uring performance flags for `eio_linux`.
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) continued work on the incremental directory reading draft PR [#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821), which received an update this week.
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) provided design feedback on [#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821), steering the API toward a non-recursive walk primitive.

latest_week.brief_summary: Stéphane Glondu reported and submitted a fix for Task.setup_pool failing hard on domain allocation errors.
latest_week.activity_summary: A new PR proposes graceful fallback in Task.setup_pool when domain allocation fails.
latest_week.activity: - [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) opened [#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/134) proposing that **`Task.setup_pool` gracefully falls back** to fewer domains when domain allocation fails, rather than raising an exception, fixing the issue reported in [#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/133); the PR touches 3 files with a small change (7 additions, 7 deletions) and is marked mergeable
latest_week.discussions_summary: Issue #133 raises a usability concern about Task.setup_pool failing hard on domain allocation errors.
latest_week.notable_discussions: - [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) filed [#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/133) arguing that **`Task.setup_pool`** should not fail entirely when it cannot allocate all requested domains, but instead fall back gracefully to however many domains it could create — a robustness improvement for environments with limited domain availability
latest_week.trends_summary: Robustness of pool initialization under resource-constrained environments is an emerging focus.
latest_week.emerging_trends: - The pairing of issue [#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/133) and PR [#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/134) reflects interest in making **`Task.setup_pool` more resilient** to runtime constraints, following a pattern of hardening the library for real-world deployment scenarios where the requested number of domains may not always be available
latest_week.contributors_summary: Stéphane Glondu opened both the issue and the corresponding fix PR this week.
latest_week.contributors: - [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) both identified and addressed the `Task.setup_pool` robustness problem, filing issue [#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/133) and immediately following up with PR [#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/134) to implement a fallback mechanism
